title: Mild Algerian Bread Flatbread
description: A yeasty, crumbly flatbread with a subtle citrus note, made with unwaxed lime and long fermentation, perfect for a low-sodium meal.
instructions: Combine flour, salt, yeast, and oregano in a large bowl.
Gradually add water while stirring until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 8 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let it ferment at room temperature for 12 hours.
After fermentation, punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal portions.
Roll each portion into a thin circle about 25 cm in diameter.
Heat a cast iron skillet over medium heat and bake each flatbread for 3 minutes on each side until golden.
Brush the cooked flatbread with olive oil and serve warm with a slice of unwaxed lime on the side.
